meta.name: Early breast cancer: ESMO Clinical Practice Guideline for diagnosis, treatment and follow-up
meta.description: Comprehensive treatment algorithm for early breast cancer (EBC) covering diagnosis, staging, locoregional treatment, and systemic therapy across all subtypes (HR+/HER2-, HER2+, TNBC).

tree.name: Early breast cancer: ESMO Clinical Practice Guideline

data.label: Early Breast Cancer (EBC)
data.content: Early breast cancer: ESMO Clinical Practice Guideline for diagnosis, treatment and follow-up
data.detail: Comprehensive ESMO recommendations for EBC covering diagnostic work-up, locoregional management (surgery + RT), and systemic therapy stratified by breast cancer subtype (HR+/HER2-, HER2+, TNBC).
data.attribute_text: The guideline provides comprehensive recommendations for the diagnosis, treatment, and follow-up of early breast cancer. Key guidance includes staging, locoregional treatment strategies, and systemic therapies for all major subtypes.

data.label: Diagnostic Work-up & Staging
data.content: Diagnostic work-up and staging of EBC.
data.detail: Bilateral mammography and ultrasound of breasts and regional lymph nodes. MRI for special cases. Core biopsy for diagnosis and biomarker assessment (ER, PgR, HER2, Ki-67). gBRCA1/2 testing if family history or therapeutic relevance. TNM staging per UICC TNM8.
data.attribute_text: The guideline provides recommendations for diagnosis and staging including bilateral mammography, ultrasound, core biopsy, biomarker assessment, and systemic staging imaging for high-risk cases.

data.label: Management of ALN Involvement
data.content: Management of ALN involvement in EBC.
data.detail: For cN0/iN0: SLNB is the standard. If SLN negative → no further locoregional Tx. If SLN positive and ACOSOG-Z0011 / AMAROS criteria met → regional RT; if not met or >2 positive SLNs → ALND. For cN+/iN+: biopsy-confirm then ALND or RT. After neoadjuvant ChT: SLNB feasible if initial cN1 converting to ycN0; persistent nodal disease requires ALND.
data.attribute_text: SLNB is recommended for clinically negative axilla. ALND remains standard for >2 positive SLNs or those not meeting Z0011 / AMAROS criteria.

data.label: EBC Treatment Overview
data.detail: HR+: ET ± OFS ± bisphosphonates ± targeted therapy. HR+/HER2-: neoadjuvant or primary surgery ± RT based on tumour size and nodal involvement. HER2+: trastuzumab ± pertuzumab-based regimens. TNBC: neoadjuvant taxane/carboplatin/anthracycline ± pembrolizumab for ≥cT2 or N+.
data.attribute_text: Overview of treatment recommendations for EBC stratified by HR and HER2 status.

data.label: Systemic Tx: HR+ / HER2-
data.content: Systemic treatment of HR-positive, HER2-negative EBC.
data.detail: Risk stratification guides systemic therapy selection. Low/intermediate risk: ET alone. Higher risk (luminal B-like, N+): ET + ChT ± CDK4/6 inhibitor (abemaciclib 2y or ribociclib 3y). gBRCA1/2m + high-risk: olaparib 1y + ET. ET is near-universal for all HR+ stages.
data.attribute_text: Treatment is individualised based on tumour stage, biology (subtype, Ki-67, grade), menopausal status, and available therapeutic interventions.

data.label: Adjuvant Endocrine Therapy in HR+ EBC
data.content: Adjuvant endocrine therapy in HR+ EBC
data.detail: ET is recommended for all patients with HR+ invasive breast cancer of any stage and HER2 status. Standard duration is 5 years; extended to 7–10 years further reduces recurrence in higher-stage disease. Choice depends on menopausal status, tumour stage/biology, and tolerance.
data.attribute_text: Adjuvant ET reduces risk of locoregional recurrence, distant metastatic recurrence, and contralateral breast cancer while improving OS. OFS + AI superior to OFS + tamoxifen in premenopausal high-risk patients.

data.label: Premenopausal
data.detail: Premenopausal status at diagnosis. OFS is required for higher-risk disease. Lower-risk patients (Luminal A-like stage I) may be managed with tamoxifen alone.
data.attribute_text: Among premenopausal women with higher-risk HR+ cancers, OFS paired with AI or tamoxifen reduces recurrence and improves OS versus tamoxifen alone.

data.label: Luminal A-like · Stage I
data.detail: Luminal A-like: ER+/PgR+, HER2-, low grade (G1–2), low Ki-67 (<20%), stage I (T1 N0). Low recurrence risk — tamoxifen monotherapy is appropriate.
data.attribute_text: Low-risk premenopausal patients with Luminal A-like stage I disease. OFS not routinely required.

data.label: Tamoxifen
data.detail: Tamoxifen 20 mg/day for 5 years (standard) or extended to 10 years for higher-risk. Well-tolerated in premenopausal women. Reduces recurrence and improves OS in HR+ EBC.
data.attribute_text: Tamoxifen is the standard adjuvant ET for premenopausal Luminal A-like stage I disease. Extended duration (7–10y) considered for higher-risk cases.

data.label:
Luminal A-like Stage II–III
Luminal B-like Stage I–III
data.content:
Luminal A-like stage II-III
Luminal B-like stage I-III
data.detail: Higher-risk premenopausal disease: Luminal A-like stage II–III or any Luminal B-like (HER2-, G2–3, Ki-67 ≥20%, or N+). OFS required in combination with AI or tamoxifen.
data.attribute_text: Higher-risk premenopausal patients require OFS. OFS + AI is superior to OFS + tamoxifen for recurrence reduction.

data.label: OFS + Tamoxifen
data.detail: Ovarian function suppression (GnRH agonist: goserelin or leuprolide) combined with tamoxifen 20 mg/day. OFS duration typically 5 years; tamoxifen continued for total 5–10 years. Option when AI is not tolerated.
data.attribute_text: OFS + tamoxifen reduces recurrences compared with tamoxifen alone in premenopausal higher-risk patients. [I, A] evidence level.

data.label: OFS + Aromatase Inhibitor (AI)
data.detail: Ovarian function suppression (GnRH agonist) + aromatase inhibitor (exemestane, anastrozole, or letrozole). OFS + AI reduces recurrences compared with OFS + tamoxifen. Preferred for highest-risk premenopausal patients.
data.attribute_text: OFS with AI reduces recurrences compared with OFS with tamoxifen. Preferred regimen for premenopausal Luminal A stage II–III or Luminal B stage I–III. [I, A].

data.label: Postmenopausal
data.detail: Postmenopausal status (natural or treatment-induced). AIs are the preferred ET backbone. OFS not required. AI-based therapy recommended for higher-risk disease.
data.attribute_text: In postmenopausal women, AIs used upfront or sequentially after 2–3 years of tamoxifen offer lower recurrence risk than tamoxifen alone, especially in higher-stage cancers.

data.label: Tamoxifen → AI
data.detail: Tamoxifen 20 mg/day for 2–3 years followed by aromatase inhibitor (anastrozole, letrozole, or exemestane) to complete 5+ years of ET. Switching strategy improves outcomes over tamoxifen alone.
data.attribute_text: Sequential tamoxifen → AI is a preferred strategy for postmenopausal women, particularly when upfront AI is not tolerated. [I, A].

data.label: Aromatase Inhibitor (AI)
data.detail: Upfront aromatase inhibitor (anastrozole, letrozole, or exemestane) for 5 years (standard) or extended to 7–10 years for higher-risk disease. AI preferred over tamoxifen in postmenopausal women with higher-risk disease.
data.attribute_text: AIs reduce recurrence risk compared with tamoxifen in postmenopausal women, especially in higher-stage cancers. Extended AI therapy (7–10y) further improves outcomes. [I, A].

data.label: AI → Tamoxifen
data.detail: Aromatase inhibitor for 2–3 years followed by tamoxifen to complete 5 years of ET. Alternative sequential strategy when extended AI is not tolerated.
data.attribute_text: AI followed by tamoxifen is an alternative sequential strategy for postmenopausal patients. [I, A].

data.label: Tamoxifen
data.detail: Tamoxifen 20 mg/day for 5 years. Recommended for postmenopausal women when AIs are contraindicated or not tolerated (e.g., severe arthralgias, osteoporosis without adequate bone protection).
data.attribute_text: Tamoxifen is acceptable for postmenopausal patients who cannot tolerate AIs. Extended duration (up to 10y) further reduces recurrence in higher-risk disease. [I, A].

data.label: Management of Early TNBC
data.detail: TNBC: ER-/PgR-/HER2-. gBRCA1/2 testing recommended for all. Systemic therapy strategy depends on clinical tumour size and nodal status. Neoadjuvant approach preferred for cT1c or larger / node-positive disease.
data.attribute_text: Treatment for early TNBC is guided by tumour size, nodal status, and germline BRCA status. Neoadjuvant immunochemotherapy (pembrolizumab + ChT) is recommended for ≥cT2 or N+.

data.label: cT1a / cT1b N0
data.detail: Very small TNBC (≤10 mm) without lymph node involvement. Primary surgery followed by whole-breast RT if BCS. Adjuvant systemic therapy depends on pathological stage.
data.attribute_text: For very small TNBC (T1a/T1b N0), primary surgery is the standard approach. Adjuvant ChT may be omitted for pT1a pN0.

data.label: Surgery → RT → ± Adjuvant ChT
data.content: Surgery + WBRT if indicated; pT1a pN0: no adjuvant systemic Tx; pT1b pN0 and >pT1b: 6-8 cycles ChT
data.detail:
• pT1a pN0: surgery ± RT; no adjuvant systemic therapy recommended
• pT1b pN0: surgery ± RT; consider 6–8 cycles adjuvant ChT (AC/EC → paclitaxel)
• >pT1b any pN: surgery ± RT + 6–8 cycles ChT
data.attribute_text: Adjuvant systemic therapy is not recommended for pT1a pN0 TNBC. For pT1b or larger, anthracycline/taxane ChT is recommended.

data.label: cT1c–T4 or N+
data.detail: Larger TNBC (>10 mm) or lymph node-positive disease. Neoadjuvant immunochemotherapy is the preferred treatment approach to enable pCR assessment and guide post-surgery therapy.
data.attribute_text: For cT1c or larger / N+ TNBC, neoadjuvant therapy is preferred. Pembrolizumab + ChT is recommended for ≥cT2 or N+ to improve pCR and event-free survival.

data.label: Neoadjuvant ChT ± Pembrolizumab
data.content: 6-8 cycles taxane-(carbo)platin-AC/EC ± pembrolizumab (≥cT2 or N+) → surgery → RT → post-neoadjuvant therapy
data.detail:
Neoadjuvant regimen:
• 6–8 cycles taxane + (carbo)platin + AC/EC
• Pembrolizumab added for ≥cT2 or N+ (KEYNOTE-522)
Post-surgery per pCR status and gBRCA:
• pCR + gBRCA1/2m → olaparib 1y
• pCR + no mutation → pembrolizumab continuation
• Residual disease + gBRCA1/2m → olaparib 1y
• Residual disease + no mutation → capecitabine 6–8 cycles
data.attribute_text: KEYNOTE-522: pembrolizumab + ChT improved pCR rate and EFS in ≥cT2/N+ TNBC. Olaparib recommended for gBRCA1/2m patients with residual disease (OlympiA trial).

data.label: Management of HER2-Positive EBC
data.detail: HER2+: IHC 3+ or ISH amplified. Trastuzumab added to ChT improves OS by ~1/3. Pertuzumab added for N+ or high-risk N0 disease. T-DM1 for residual disease after neoadjuvant HP + ChT. HR+ patients also receive adjuvant ET.
data.attribute_text: Trastuzumab-based therapy is standard for all HER2+ EBC. Dual HER2 blockade (HP) preferred for higher-risk disease. T-DM1 recommended for non-pCR after neoadjuvant HP-ChT.

data.label: cT1 cN0
data.detail: Small HER2+ tumour (≤2 cm) with clinically negative lymph nodes. Primary surgery is the standard. Tumours <2 cm may also be considered for neoadjuvant therapy. Adjuvant therapy based on final pathological stage.
data.attribute_text: For small HER2+ tumours (cT1 cN0), primary surgery is performed. Adjuvant paclitaxel + trastuzumab (12 weeks) is recommended for pT1 pN0.

data.label: Surgery + RT → Adjuvant HER2-Targeted Tx
data.content: Surgery + RT if indicated → pT1 pN0: paclitaxel × 12w + trastuzumab 18 cycles; ≥pT2 pN0 or pN+: 6-8 cycles ChT + trastuzumab or HP; all complete 1y trastuzumab or HP; HR+: add ET
data.detail:
• pT1 pN0: paclitaxel × 12 weeks + trastuzumab (total 18 cycles = 1 year)
• ≥pT2 pN0: 6–8 cycles ChT + trastuzumab (total 1 year)
• pN+: 6–8 cycles ChT + HP (total 1 year)
• All: complete 1 year trastuzumab or HP
• HR+: adjuvant ET (AI or tamoxifen per menopausal status)
data.attribute_text: Adjuvant paclitaxel + trastuzumab (APT regimen) for pT1 pN0 HER2+ EBC. Anthracycline/taxane + HP for ≥pT2 or N+ disease.

data.label: ≥cT2 or cN+
data.detail: Larger HER2+ tumours (>2 cm) or lymph node-positive disease. Neoadjuvant dual HER2 blockade (trastuzumab + pertuzumab) with ChT is the standard. Post-surgery therapy depends on pCR status.
data.attribute_text: Neoadjuvant HP + ChT is standard for ≥cT2 or cN+ HER2+ EBC. pCR guides post-surgery therapy: T-DM1 for residual disease improves iDFS (KATHERINE trial).

data.label: Neoadjuvant ChT + HP → Surgery + RT → Post-neoadjuvant Tx
data.sublabel: pCR: 1y T or HP · Residual: T-DM1 ≤14 cycles
data.content: 6-8 cycles ChT + HP → surgery → RT if indicated → pCR: 1y trastuzumab or HP; residual disease: T-DM1 ≤14 cycles; HR+: add ET
data.detail:
Neoadjuvant: 6–8 cycles anthracycline/taxane + trastuzumab + pertuzumab (HP)
Post-surgery:
• pCR (ypT0/is ypN0):
  - cN0 at diagnosis → 1 year trastuzumab
  - cN+ at diagnosis → 1 year HP
• Residual invasive disease (non-pCR):
  - T-DM1 (trastuzumab emtansine) up to 14 cycles
  - Neratinib 1 year for HR+ with residual disease (optional)
• HR+: adjuvant ET regardless of pCR status
data.attribute_text: KATHERINE trial: T-DM1 superior to trastuzumab for non-pCR after neoadjuvant HP + ChT (iDFS HR 0.50). APHINITY: pertuzumab + trastuzumab + ChT improved iDFS for N+ HER2+ EBC.
